overall_justification: While the majority of the newspaper edition focuses on other topics, the mentions related to the "Caixa de Conversão" lean towards an Orthodox perspective. The "Pingos e Hespingos" section includes a subtle jab at representatives from Minas who voted for the Caixa and protectionism, suggesting disapproval of these policies.  Additionally, the comment about politicians potentially setting the exchange rate even lower than 15d (the rate generally favored by expansionists) implies a critical view of manipulating the currency, aligning with Orthodox concerns about fiscal probity. The context of these mentions, within a section known for its satirical and critical tone, reinforces the interpretation of a skeptical, Orthodox-leaning stance.

supporting_evidence.quote: Illustre editor—Não senhor. O drama Os mythos da desgraça, do sr. Quintino Bocayuva, foi escripto ha muitos annos, e não tem, portanto, a menor allusão aos representantes de Minas que votaram pela Caixa de Conversão e pelo proteccionismo de escacho peccgueiro.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote, from the 'Pingos e Hespingos' section, subtly criticizes representatives who voted for the Caixa and protectionism, suggesting an Orthodox leaning.

supporting_evidence.quote:
Os politicos são homens sem palavra, não cumprem as promessas.
Não é assim... As vezes fazem mais do que o que prometiam.
Nunca vi isso!
Está-se a ver agora mesmo- Promettem pôr o cambio a 15...
— Sim... E vão pol-o a 16 ou menos... Como á os da gente.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This exchange, also from 'Pingos e Hespingos,' expresses skepticism about politicians' promises regarding the exchange rate and implies a potential devaluation beyond even what expansionists advocate, aligning with Orthodox concerns.
